title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: bridgr mixed-frequency forecasting package bridge models, MIDAS-style regressions, intermediate specifications estimate within-period weights data. core workflow always : Provide one lower-frequency target series one higher-frequency indicators. Decide missing indicator observations handled indic_predict. Decide indicators aligned target frequency indic_aggregators. Fit target equation bridge(), inspect summary(), produce target-period forecasts forecast(). vignette walks workflow package’s built-Swiss GDP barometer data.

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: gdp_growth quarterly, baro monthly. bridge() recognizes frequency mismatch automatically aligns indicator target frequency fitting target equation.

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: default mean aggregator classic bridge-model setup: monthly block completed first, averaged quarterly frequency target equation estimated. fitted object stores aligned data went estimation future target-period regressor path used forecasting.
code: bridge_model <- bridge(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,   target_lags = 1,   h = 2 )  forecast(bridge_model) #> Bridge forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Target model: fc_model #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   time        mean    se lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #>   <date>     <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01 0.972    NA       NA       NA       NA       NA #> 2 2023-04-01 0.698    NA       NA       NA       NA       NA tail(bridge_model$estimation_set) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   time       gdp_growth  baro baro_lag1 #>   <date>          <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 2021-07-01      2.34  112.      125.  #> 2 2021-10-01      0.411 104.      112.  #> 3 2022-01-01      0.105  97.4     104.  #> 4 2022-04-01      1.03   94.3      97.4 #> 5 2022-07-01      0.255  90.0      94.3 #> 6 2022-10-01      0.102  90.7      90.0 bridge_model$forecast_set #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   time        baro baro_lag1 #>   <date>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01  97.4      90.7 #> 2 2023-04-01  99.8      97.4

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: summary() forecast() use stable package-specific layout. base output across bridge, MIDAS-style, direct-alignment specifications. Additional details, optimization summaries bootstrap settings, appended relevant.
code: summary(bridge_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Target model: fc_model #> Estimation rows: 74 #> Regressors: baro, baro_lag1 #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   term      estimate bootstrap_se #>   <chr>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 ar1         0.243            NA #> 2 intercept  -6.49             NA #> 3 baro        0.161            NA #> 4 baro_lag1  -0.0917           NA #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   indicator frequency      predict    aggregation indicator_model #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>      <chr>       <chr>           #> 1 baro      month (step 1) auto.arima mean        fc_model        #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Method: none #> -----------------------------------

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: package also provides built-plotting method fitted bridge models. type = "forecast", shows observed target history together forecast path generated model.
code: plot(bridge_model, type = "forecast")

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: set indic_predict = "direct", bridgr switches indicator forecasting direct MIDAS-style alignment. case, latest complete high-frequency blocks assigned backward target periods instead forecast forward first. particularly useful ragged edge want work observed high-frequency information avoid separate indicator forecasting step.
code: direct_model <- bridge(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "direct",   indic_aggregators = "unrestricted",   h = 1 )  forecast(direct_model) #> Bridge forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Target model: lm #> Indicator handling: direct alignment #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   time        mean    se lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #>   <date>     <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01 0.522    NA       NA       NA       NA       NA

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: default, bridgr returns point forecasts . want uncertainty output, request estimation time se = TRUE bootstrap configuration.  current uncertainty implementation conditional block bootstrap final target-frequency estimation sample. re-estimate indicator forecast models parametric aggregation weights inside bootstrap draw. se = TRUE, plot(..., type = "forecast") automatically adds bootstrap forecast interval top historical target path forecast mean.
code: boot_model <- bridge(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   target_lags = 1,   h = 4,   se = TRUE,   bootstrap = list(type = "block", N = 40, block_length = NULL) )  forecast(boot_model) #> Bridge forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 4 #> Target model: fc_model #> Uncertainty: conditional block bootstrap #> Bootstrap draws: 40 / 40 #> Block length: 5 #> ----------------------------------- #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>   time        mean    se lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #>   <date>     <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01 0.116 0.234 -0.00117    0.465   -0.369    0.878 #> 2 2023-04-01 0.428 0.115  0.315      0.615    0.203    0.673 #> 3 2023-07-01 0.487 0.103  0.389      0.663    0.339    0.705 #> 4 2023-10-01 0.509 0.103  0.401      0.676    0.358    0.743 summary(boot_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 4 #> Target model: fc_model #> Estimation rows: 75 #> Regressors: baro #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   term      estimate bootstrap_se #>   <chr>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 ar1        -0.135        0.209  #> 2 intercept  -9.06         3.32   #> 3 baro        0.0950       0.0330 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   indicator frequency      predict    aggregation indicator_model #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>      <chr>       <chr>           #> 1 baro      month (step 1) auto.arima mean        fc_model        #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Method: conditional block bootstrap #> Bootstrap draws: 40 / 40 #> Block length: 5 #> ----------------------------------- plot(boot_model, type = "forecast")

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: vignette vignette("mixed-frequency-modeling", package = "bridgr") compares main aggregation strategies shows bridgr moves classic bridge models unrestricted parametric MIDAS-style specifications. vignette vignette("ragged-edge-nowcasting", package = "bridgr") focuses indic_predict different ways handle incomplete high-frequency data forecast origin. vignette vignette("uncertainty--scenarios", package = "bridgr") shows work conditional block-bootstrap uncertainty scenario forecasts based custom future regressor paths.

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: bridgr supports several ways map higher-frequency observations lower-frequency target equation. choices span continuum: Bridge-style deterministic aggregation: "mean", "last", "sum", fixed numeric weights. Unrestricted mixed-frequency regression: "unrestricted". Parametric MIDAS-style weighting: "expalmon", "beta", "legendre". vignette uses simple monthly--quarterly simulation differences approaches easy see.

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: target intentionally driven middle month quarter, can see different aggregation schemes react. monthly indicator also slot-specific within-quarter movements, unrestricted specification can estimate three distinct monthly coefficients.
code: n_quarters <- 40 quarter_index <- rep(seq_len(n_quarters), each = 3) slot <- rep(1:3, times = n_quarters) monthly_time <- seq(   as.Date("2010-01-01"),   by = "month",   length.out = n_quarters * 3 ) monthly_indicator <- dplyr::tibble(   time = monthly_time,   value = 15 + quarter_index * 0.35 +     ifelse(slot == 1, 0.8 * sin(quarter_index / 2), 0) +     ifelse(slot == 2, -0.6 * cos(quarter_index / 3), 0) +     ifelse(slot == 3, 0.7 * sin(quarter_index / 4 + 0.3), 0) )  quarter_time <- monthly_time[seq(1, length(monthly_time), by = 3)] quarter_target <- dplyr::tibble(   time = quarter_time,   value = 0.5 +     vapply(       seq_along(quarter_time),       function(i) {         block <- monthly_indicator$value[((i - 1) * 3 + 1):(i * 3)]         0.2 * block[[1]] + 0.6 * block[[2]] + 0.2 * block[[3]]       },       numeric(1)     ) +     rep(c(0.1, -0.05, 0.08, -0.02), length.out = length(quarter_time)) )

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: classic bridge-model choices. easy interpret often work well want transparent rule within-period aggregation.
code: mean_model <- bridge(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   h = 1 )  last_model <- bridge(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "last",   h = 1 )  summary(mean_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: quarter_target #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Target model: lm #> Estimation rows: 40 #> Regressors: monthly_indicator #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   term              estimate bootstrap_se #>   <chr>                <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)          0.516           NA #> 2 monthly_indicator    0.999           NA #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   indicator         frequency      predict aggregation indicator_model #>   <chr>             <chr>          <chr>   <chr>       <chr>           #> 1 monthly_indicator month (step 1) last    mean        deterministic   #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Method: none #> ----------------------------------- summary(last_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: quarter_target #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Target model: lm #> Estimation rows: 40 #> Regressors: monthly_indicator #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   term              estimate bootstrap_se #>   <chr>                <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)          0.590           NA #> 2 monthly_indicator    0.993           NA #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   indicator         frequency      predict aggregation indicator_model #>   <chr>             <chr>          <chr>   <chr>       <chr>           #> 1 monthly_indicator month (step 1) last    last        deterministic   #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Method: none #> -----------------------------------

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: "unrestricted" estimates one coefficient within-quarter monthly observation. monthly--quarterly example means three separate regressors. example keeps indic_predict = "last" comparison isolates aggregation choice; combining "unrestricted" indic_predict = "direct" gives direct MIDAS-style alignment covered ragged-edge vignette.
code: unrestricted_model <- bridge(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "unrestricted",   h = 1 )  summary(unrestricted_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: quarter_target #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Target model: lm #> Estimation rows: 40 #> Regressors: monthly_indicator_hf1, monthly_indicator_hf2, monthly_indicator_hf3 #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   term                  estimate bootstrap_se #>   <chr>                    <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)              0.541           NA #> 2 monthly_indicator_hf1    0.198           NA #> 3 monthly_indicator_hf2    0.596           NA #> 4 monthly_indicator_hf3    0.205           NA #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   indicator         frequency      predict aggregation  indicator_model #>   <chr>             <chr>          <chr>   <chr>        <chr>           #> 1 monthly_indicator month (step 1) last    unrestricted deterministic   #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Method: none #> -----------------------------------

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: bridgr also supports parametric weighting rules estimate within-period shape data keeping number free parameters small. fitted object stores estimated weight profile underlying parametric coefficients.
code: expalmon_model <- bridge(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "expalmon",   solver_options = list(seed = 123, n_starts = 1, maxiter = 100),   h = 1 )  beta_model <- bridge(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "beta",   solver_options = list(     seed = 123,     n_starts = 1,     maxiter = 100,     start_values = c(2, 2)   ),   h = 1 )  legendre_model <- bridge(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "legendre",   solver_options = list(seed = 123, n_starts = 1, maxiter = 100),   h = 1 ) indicator_id <- expalmon_model$indic_name[[1]]  dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::tibble(     model = "expalmon",     month = seq_along(expalmon_model$parametric_weights[[indicator_id]]),     weight = expalmon_model$parametric_weights[[indicator_id]]   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "beta",     month = seq_along(beta_model$parametric_weights[[indicator_id]]),     weight = beta_model$parametric_weights[[indicator_id]]   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "legendre",     month = seq_along(legendre_model$parametric_weights[[indicator_id]]),     weight = legendre_model$parametric_weights[[indicator_id]]   ) ) #> # A tibble: 9 × 3 #>   model    month    weight #>   <chr>    <int>     <dbl> #> 1 expalmon     1 1.99 e- 1 #> 2 expalmon     2 5.97 e- 1 #> 3 expalmon     3 2.05 e- 1 #> 4 beta         1 8.88 e-16 #> 5 beta         2 1.000e+ 0 #> 6 beta         3 8.88 e-16 #> 7 legendre     1 1.99 e- 1 #> 8 legendre     2 5.97 e- 1 #> 9 legendre     3 2.05 e- 1

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: models share downstream interface.
code: dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::tibble(     model = "mean",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(mean_model)$mean)   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "last",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(last_model)$mean)   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "unrestricted",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(unrestricted_model)$mean)   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "expalmon",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(expalmon_model)$mean)   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "beta",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(beta_model)$mean)   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "legendre",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(legendre_model)$mean)   ) ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   model        forecast #>   <chr>           <dbl> #> 1 mean             29.0 #> 2 last             28.9 #> 3 unrestricted     29.0 #> 4 expalmon         29.0 #> 5 beta             29.0 #> 6 legendre         29.0

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: rough guide: Use deterministic bridge aggregation want transparent stable nowcasting rule. Use "unrestricted" frequency gap small want within-period observation coefficient. Use "expalmon", "beta", "legendre" want data-driven within-period weights like parsimonious parameterization "unrestricted". Use indic_predict = "direct" want direct MIDAS-style alignment based latest observed high-frequency blocks. key design choice bridgr specifications share estimation, forecasting, summary workflow. can therefore move bridge MIDAS-style models without switching different API.

title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: Mixed-frequency nowcasting usually happens ragged edge: lower-frequency target released yet, higher-frequency indicators partially observed current target period. bridgr handles situation indic_predict, controls missing high-frequency tail completed target equation estimated forecast. currently supported options : "last": extend latest available high-frequency observation. "mean": extend mean latest available high-frequency block. "auto.arima": fit automatic ARIMA model indicator forecast missing observations. "ets": fit ETS model indicator forecast missing observations. "direct": forecast indicator . Instead, align latest observed complete high-frequency blocks directly target periods.

title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: start package’s quarterly GDP growth series monthly barometer, remove last quarterly GDP observation last two monthly indicator observations. creates clean nowcast setup: final quarter now forecasted rather estimated, monthly indicator partially observed forecast quarter. target history now ends one quarter earlier, monthly indicator contains partial block quarter want nowcast.

title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: model specification case. difference missing monthly observations completed indicator aggregated quarterly frequency.
code: predict_methods <- c("last", "mean", "auto.arima", "ets")  models <- lapply(   predict_methods,   function(method) {     bridge(       target = gdp_nowcast,       indic = baro_ragged,       indic_predict = method,       indic_aggregators = "mean",       target_lags = 1,       h = 1     )   } ) names(models) <- predict_methods  dplyr::bind_rows(   lapply(     predict_methods,     function(method) {       fc <- forecast(models[[method]])       dplyr::tibble(         method = method,         forecast_time = fc$time,         forecast = as.numeric(fc$mean)       )     }   ) ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   method     forecast_time forecast #>   <chr>      <date>           <dbl> #> 1 last       2022-10-01      -0.885 #> 2 mean       2022-10-01      -0.810 #> 3 auto.arima 2022-10-01      -0.670 #> 4 ets        2022-10-01      -0.884

what: Looking at the Forecast Regressor Set
title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: easiest way see method inspect forecast_set, stores target-period regressors used final forecast. "last", last observed monthly value extended forward. "mean", mean latest available high-frequency block extended forward instead.
code: lapply(   models[c("last", "mean")],   function(model) {     model$forecast_set   } ) #> $last #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   time       baro_ragged #>   <date>           <dbl> #> 1 2022-10-01        87.5 #>  #> $mean #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   time       baro_ragged #>   <date>           <dbl> #> 1 2022-10-01        88.2

title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: "direct" takes different route. indicator forecasted. Instead, bridgr assigns latest complete high-frequency blocks backward target periods. closest direct MIDAS-style setup. especially useful want avoid separate indicator forecasting step prefer work observed high-frequency data.
code: direct_model <- bridge(   target = gdp_nowcast,   indic = baro_ragged,   indic_predict = "direct",   indic_aggregators = "unrestricted",   h = 1 )  forecast(direct_model) #> Bridge forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_nowcast #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Target model: lm #> Indicator handling: direct alignment #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   time         mean    se lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #>   <date>      <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2022-10-01 -0.366    NA       NA       NA       NA       NA

title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: rough guide: Use "last" simple carry-forward rule acceptable. Use "mean" want stable deterministic fill based latest high-frequency block. Use "auto.arima" "ets" indicator meaningful time-series structure separate extrapolation step sensible. Use "direct" want direct MIDAS-style alignment without indicator forecasting. good part downstream workflow change. summary() forecast() interface works across nowcasting choices.

title: Bootstrap Uncertainty and Scenario Forecasting
text: bridgr can return point forecasts , can also compute conditional bootstrap uncertainty fitted target equation. relevant estimation arguments : se = FALSE: point forecasts . se = TRUE: compute conditional bootstrap uncertainty. bootstrap = list(type = "block", N = 100, block_length = NULL): control bootstrap. moment, "block" supported bootstrap type.

what: Fitting a Model with Bootstrap Uncertainty
title: Bootstrap Uncertainty and Scenario Forecasting
text: current implementation uses conditional block bootstrap final target-frequency estimation sample. re-estimate indicator forecast models parametric aggregation weights inside bootstrap draw.
code: gdp_growth <- suppressMessages(tsbox::ts_na_omit(tsbox::ts_pc(gdp)))  boot_model <- bridge(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,   target_lags = 1,   h = 2,   se = TRUE,   bootstrap = list(type = "block", N = 20, block_length = NULL) )

title: Bootstrap Uncertainty and Scenario Forecasting
text: model estimated se = TRUE, forecast() returns standardized forecast object : mean se lower upper forecast_set bootstrap metadata intervals empirical bootstrap intervals based stored bootstrap forecast draws.
code: fc <- forecast(boot_model)  fc #> Bridge forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Target model: fc_model #> Uncertainty: conditional block bootstrap #> Bootstrap draws: 20 / 20 #> Block length: 5 #> ----------------------------------- #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   time        mean    se lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #>   <date>     <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01 0.972 0.325    0.339    1.24     0.302    1.25  #> 2 2023-04-01 0.698 0.151    0.474    0.873    0.402    0.881 fc$bootstrap #> $enabled #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $type #> [1] "block" #>  #> $N #> [1] 20 #>  #> $valid_N #> [1] 20 #>  #> $block_length #> [1] 5 #>  #> $conditional #> [1] TRUE

title: Bootstrap Uncertainty and Scenario Forecasting
text: bootstrap run also feeds summary(). printed summary keeps base layout point-estimate model adds uncertainty section bootstrap output available.
code: summary(boot_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Target model: fc_model #> Estimation rows: 74 #> Regressors: baro, baro_lag1 #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   term      estimate bootstrap_se #>   <chr>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 ar1         0.243        0.108  #> 2 intercept  -6.49         1.56   #> 3 baro        0.161        0.0456 #> 4 baro_lag1  -0.0917       0.0409 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   indicator frequency      predict    aggregation indicator_model #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>      <chr>       <chr>           #> 1 baro      month (step 1) auto.arima mean        fc_model        #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Method: conditional block bootstrap #> Bootstrap draws: 20 / 20 #> Block length: 5 #> -----------------------------------

title: Bootstrap Uncertainty and Scenario Forecasting
text: want forecast target different future regressor path, pass custom xreg object forecast(). custom regressor names must match ones used fitted target equation. model contains stored bootstrap draws, forecast(..., xreg = ...) reuses bootstrap refits evaluates scenario regressor path. keeps uncertainty handling consistent baseline forecast scenario forecasts.
code: scenario_xreg <- dplyr::tibble(   id = rep(boot_model$regressor_names, each = nrow(boot_model$forecast_set)),   time = rep(     boot_model$forecast_set$time,     times = length(boot_model$regressor_names)   ),   value = c(     boot_model$forecast_set$baro + 5,     boot_model$forecast_set$baro_lag1 + 5   ) )  scenario_fc <- forecast(boot_model, xreg = scenario_xreg)  dplyr::tibble(   time = fc$time,   baseline = as.numeric(fc$mean),   scenario = as.numeric(scenario_fc$mean) ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   time       baseline scenario #>   <date>        <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01    0.972     1.32 #> 2 2023-04-01    0.698     1.05

title: Bootstrap Uncertainty and Scenario Forecasting
text: interested point estimates, leave se = FALSE. case, bootstrap ignored forecast() still returns object shape, NA uncertainty fields.
code: point_model <- bridge(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,   target_lags = 1,   h = 1,   se = FALSE,   bootstrap = list(type = "block", N = 20) )  forecast(point_model) #> Bridge forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Target model: fc_model #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   time        mean    se lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #>   <date>     <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01 0.972    NA       NA       NA       NA       NA

title: Bootstrap Uncertainty and Scenario Forecasting
text: intervals conditional aligned target-frequency design matrix. means naturally interpreted uncertainty final target equation given regressor path, rather full end--end uncertainty whole mixed-frequency system.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Burri M (2026). bridgr: Bridging Data Frequencies Timely Economic Forecasts. R package version 0.1.2, https://github.com/marcburri/bridgr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {bridgr: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts},   author = {Marc Burri},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.2},   url = {https://github.com/marcburri/bridgr}, }

what: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
title: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
text: bridgr designed simplify implementation evaluation bridge models, useful nowcasting (predicting present near-term) forecasting macroeconomic variables like GDP. Bridge models statistical tools link high-frequency indicators (e.g., monthly industrial production) low-frequency target variables (e.g., quarterly GDP) forecasting aggregating indicators match target’s frequency. enable timely predictions official release low-frequency data, making essential policymakers need early insights decision-making. bridgr supports regular frequency ladders second year, several deterministic model-based indicator forecasting rules, multiple aggregation choices including joint expalmon weighting. target period contains high-frequency observations implied current frequency mapping, package keeps recent observations reports summarized warning.

title: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
text: CRAN: can install development version bridgr like :
code: install.packages("bridgr") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("marcburri/bridgr")

title: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
text: basic example default mean aggregation: want data-driven within-period weights instead simple mean, can switch indicator aggregator expalmon. corresponding weights estimated jointly within bridge model, solver_options let control optimization.
code: library(bridgr)  gdp <- suppressMessages(tsbox::ts_na_omit(tsbox::ts_pc(bridgr::gdp)))  bridge_model <- bridge(   target = gdp,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 2,   target_lags = 1,   h = 2 ) #> [value]: 'values'  #> [value]: 'values'  forecast(bridge_model) #> Bridge forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Target model: fc_model #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   time        mean    se lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #>   <date>     <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01 0.831    NA       NA       NA       NA       NA #> 2 2023-04-01 0.536    NA       NA       NA       NA       NA  summary(bridge_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Target model: fc_model #> Estimation rows: 73 #> Regressors: baro, baro_lag1, baro_lag2 #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   term      estimate bootstrap_se #>   <chr>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 ar1         0.174            NA #> 2 intercept  -7.42             NA #> 3 baro        0.157            NA #> 4 baro_lag1  -0.0957           NA #> 5 baro_lag2   0.0172           NA #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   indicator frequency      predict    aggregation indicator_model #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>      <chr>       <chr>           #> 1 baro      month (step 1) auto.arima mean        fc_model        #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Method: none #> ----------------------------------- expalmon_model <- bridge(   target = gdp,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "expalmon",   solver_options = list(seed = 123, n_starts = 3),   h = 1 ) #> [value]: 'values'  #> [value]: 'values'  summary(expalmon_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Target model: lm #> Estimation rows: 75 #> Regressors: baro #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   term        estimate bootstrap_se #>   <chr>          <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)  -9.37             NA #> 2 baro          0.0982           NA #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   indicator frequency      predict    aggregation indicator_model #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>      <chr>       <chr>           #> 1 baro      month (step 1) auto.arima expalmon    fc_model        #> ----------------------------------- #> Estimated parametric aggregation: #> baro weights: 0.006, 0.994, 0 #> baro parameters: -4.914, -10 #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Method: none #> ----------------------------------- #> Joint parametric aggregation optimization: #> Method: L-BFGS-B #> Objective value: 60.8316 #> Convergence code: 0 #> Best start: 1 / 3 #> Message: CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH #> -----------------------------------

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
text: Estimate bridge model links one lower-frequency target series one higher-frequency indicator series. Indicators aligned target frequency forecasting missing higher-frequency observations aggregating within target period.

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
code: bridge(   target,   indic,   indic_predict = NULL,   indic_aggregators = NULL,   indic_lags = 0,   target_lags = 0,   h = 1,   frequency_conversions = NULL,   se = FALSE,   bootstrap = NULL,   solver_options = NULL,   ... )

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
text: target single target series tsbox::ts_boxable() format. indic One indicator series tsbox::ts_boxable() format. indic_predict character vector indicator forecasting methods. Length must 1 equal number indicator series. Setting indic_predict = "direct" switches direct MIDAS-style alignment: indicators forecasted, recent complete high-frequency blocks assigned backward target periods. Direct alignment must used indicators . h > 1, latest complete block assigned farthest requested forecast horizon earlier complete blocks assigned backward . indic_predict = "mean", missing high-frequency observations filled mean latest available obs_per_target high-frequency observations, mean extended across forecast horizon. indic_aggregators character vector aggregation methods list numeric weights. Length must 1 equal number indicator series. Numeric weights must sum one appropriate length inferred target-period block size. "unrestricted" keeps one separate coefficient per high-frequency observation within target period. parametric aggregators use two coefficients : "expalmon" uses (linear, quadratic), "beta" uses (left_shape, right_shape) normalized beta shape parameters, "legendre" uses (first_order, second_order) coefficients first two shifted orthonormal Legendre basis functions. indic_lags non-negative integer giving number target-period lags add aggregated indicator. target_lags non-negative integer giving autoregressive order target equation. h positive integer forecast horizon measured target periods. frequency_conversions named numeric vector used customize regular frequency ladder. Supported names spm, mph, hpd, dpw, wpm, mpq, qpy. se Logical flag indicating whether conditional bootstrap standard errors forecast intervals computed. FALSE, bootstrap ignored. bootstrap list bootstrap controls. Currently list(type = "block", N = 100, block_length = NULL) supported. type must "block", N number bootstrap replications, block_length target-frequency block length. block_length NULL, bridge() uses ceiling(n^(1/3)) based final target-period estimation sample size. bootstrap conditional aligned low-frequency design matrix therefore re-estimate indicator forecasts aggregation weights. solver_options list optional controls joint parametric-weight optimization. Supported entries : method optimizer ("L-BFGS-B", "BFGS", "Nelder-Mead", "nlminb"), maxiter iteration budget per optimization run, n_starts number multi-start attempts, seed reproducible random restarts, trace optimizer verbosity, start_values user-supplied initial parameter values. start_values can either numeric vector named list. numeric vector, values concatenated indicator order across parametric aggregators. Within indicator, parameter order (linear, quadratic) "expalmon", (left_shape, right_shape) "beta", (first_order, second_order) "legendre". Named-list start_values must provide exactly required number values parametric indicator. controls ignored unless least one indicator uses parametric aggregator. ... Reserved future extensions.

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
text: object class "bridge" containing standardized input series, inferred frequencies, aligned estimation forecast datasets, fitted target model, fitted indicator models, metadata required forecast.bridge() summary.bridge().

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
text: Supported indicator forecasting methods "mean", "last", "auto.arima", "ets", "direct". Supported aggregation methods "mean", "last", "sum", "unrestricted", "expalmon", "beta", "legendre", numeric weight vector supplied inside list(). "unrestricted" expands high-frequency observation within target period bridge regressor, corresponds U-MIDAS style specification frequency gap small. one indicators use parametric aggregator, corresponding aggregation weights estimated jointly final bridge-model objective rather one indicator time. Unrestricted mixed-frequency regressions can become parameter-heavy quickly. indic_aggregators = "unrestricted", bridge() warns final estimation sample contains fewer 10 observations per predictor bridge regression. package assumes regular frequency ladder second -> minute -> hour -> day -> week -> month -> quarter -> year. default number lower-level observations per higher-level unit : spm = 60 mph = 60 hpd = 24 dpw = 7 wpm = 4 mpq = 3 qpy = 4 Users can override subset values frequency_conversions. target period contains high-frequency observations implied current mapping, bridge() keeps recent observations emits summarized warning. target period contains fewer observations required, call fails. Month-, quarter-, year-based input dates standardized period starts needed frequency recognition.

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
text: Baffigi, ., Golinelli, R., & Parigi, G. (2004). Bridge models forecast euro area GDP. International Journal Forecasting, 20(3), 447-460. doi:10.1016/S0169-2070(03)00067-0 Ghysels, E., Sinko, ., & Valkanov, R. (2007). MIDAS regressions: results new directions. Econometric Reviews, 26(1), 53-90. doi:10.1080/07474930600972467 Andreou, E., Ghysels, E., & Kourtellos, . (2010). Regression models mixed sampling frequencies. Journal Econometrics, 158(2), 246-261. doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2010.01.004 Schumacher, C. (2016). comparison MIDAS bridge equations. International Journal Forecasting, 32(2), 257-270. doi:10.1016/j.ijforecast.2015.07.004 Burri, M. (2026). Nowcasting Swiss GDP Growth Public Lead Texts: Simple Methods Sufficient. Oxford Bulletin Economics Statistics, 1-25. doi:10.1111/obes.70073

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
code: gdp_growth <- suppressMessages(tsbox::ts_na_omit(tsbox::ts_pc(gdp)))  model <- bridge(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,   target_lags = 1,   h = 2 )  expalmon_model <- bridge(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "expalmon",   solver_options = list(seed = 123, n_starts = 3),   h = 1 )  forecast(model) #> Bridge forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Target model: fc_model #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   time        mean    se lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #>   <date>     <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01 0.972    NA       NA       NA       NA       NA #> 2 2023-04-01 0.698    NA       NA       NA       NA       NA summary(expalmon_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Target model: lm #> Estimation rows: 75 #> Regressors: baro #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   term        estimate bootstrap_se #>   <chr>          <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)  -9.37             NA #> 2 baro          0.0982           NA #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   indicator frequency      predict    aggregation indicator_model #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>      <chr>       <chr>           #> 1 baro      month (step 1) auto.arima expalmon    fc_model        #> ----------------------------------- #> Estimated parametric aggregation: #> baro weights: 0.006, 0.994, 0 #> baro parameters: -4.914, -10 #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Method: none #> ----------------------------------- #> Joint parametric aggregation optimization: #> Method: L-BFGS-B #> Objective value: 60.8316 #> Convergence code: 0 #> Best start: 1 / 3 #> Message: CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH #> -----------------------------------

title: Forecast a Bridge Model — forecast.bridge
text: object "bridge" object returned bridge(). xreg Optional future regressors tsbox::ts_boxable() format. omitted, forecast set stored inside object used. supplied, xreg must contain regressor names used fitting bridge equation. level Confidence levels used bootstrap forecast intervals model estimated se = TRUE. uncertainty unavailable, forecast() still returns se, lower, upper components, filled NA. ... Unused. x "bridge_forecast" object returned forecast.bridge().

title: Forecast a Bridge Model — forecast.bridge
text: object class "bridge_forecast" "forecast" containing point forecasts, conditional bootstrap uncertainty summaries, target-period regressors used forecasting, bootstrap metadata. x, invisibly.

title: Swiss Economic Indicators — gdp
text: collection datasets containing economic indicators Switzerland.

title: Swiss Economic Indicators — gdp
code: gdp  baro  wea  fcurve

title: Swiss Economic Indicators — gdp
text: baro: KOF barometer, monthly business cycle indicator. fcurve: F-curve, daily business cycle indicator. gdp: Quarterly GDP data (real, seasonally adjusted). wea: Weekly Economic Activity (WEA) indicator.

title: Swiss Economic Indicators — gdp
text: baro: Source: KOF Swiss Economic Institute Timeframe: January 2004 - December 2022 Frequency: Monthly Format: tibble monthly observations 2 variables: time: Date, month year observation. values: Numeric, value KOF barometer. fcurve: Source: Burri Kaufmann GitHub Timeframe: January 2004 - December 2022 Frequency: Daily Format: tibble daily observations 2 variables: time: Date, date observation. values: Numeric, value F-curve (inverted compatibility). gdp: Source: SECO Timeframe: January 2004 - December 2022 Frequency: Quarterly Format: tibble quarterly observations 2 variables: time: Date, quarter year observation. values: Numeric, real GDP (seasonally adjusted). wea: Source: SECO WEA Indicator Timeframe: January 2005 - December 2022 Frequency: Weekly Format: tibble weekly observations 2 variables: time: Date, week year observation. values: Numeric, value WEA.

title: Plot a Bridge Model — plot.bridge
text: x "bridge" object returned bridge(). type Plot type. Use "forecast" plot observed target history together bridge forecast, "fit" compare observed target -sample fitted values. level Forecast interval level passed forecast.bridge() type = "forecast". history_n Number historical target observations display. Defaults recent 50. Set NULL show full history. xlab, ylab, main Optional axis title labels. omitted, sensible defaults chosen type. ... Additional arguments passed theme_bridgr().

title: bridgr 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-18 Solve dependency issues xts

title: bridgr 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-13 Added gdp,baro, wea fcurve datasets. Added bridge(), forecast() summary() functions. Supports target variables monthly, quarterly yearly frequency, indicator variables daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly yearly frequency. Supports auto.arima, ets methods indicator variable forecasting. Supports aggregation indicator variables match target’s frequency using custom weighting functions, exponential Almon polynomials methods.
